_synthetic: Committed demo/test fixture. Every name, unit, and number is invented — no real tenant data (§6.6). The `live` block below is HAND-COMPUTED from `records` so the §6.4 invariant test is not circular. 'Casey Nguyen' has property_id null on purpose: the unmapped-eviction case (no numeric property id exists anywhere in the on-disk rent_roll/delinquency CSVs, same gap as renewals). 'Taylor Reed' and 'Morgan Hale' have no delinquency match (last_payment null, balance_source rent_roll_past_due) — the edge case where an Evict row is still shown with a real balance even though the delinquency join missed it, never dropped. 'Casey Lane' reuses the entities.json resident (property 101, '1102 Moss Dr Unit A') so a resident-scope test has a real eviction to match. oldest_eviction_age_days (65) is the max age among records that DO have a last_payment date (Jordan Blake, Sam Ortiz, Casey Lane) — Taylor/Morgan/Casey-N contribute no age (null last_payment), mirroring aggregateTurns' oldest_days-from-move_out convention.

delinquency_join.why: 3/6 eviction rows matched delinquency.csv by (property, tenant name); the other 3 fall back to the rent_roll Past Due column (Taylor Reed, Morgan Hale) or have no source at all (Casey Nguyen, the unmapped case); see pulse_evictions_metrics.py's module docstring for the real on-disk finding this fixture demonstrates.

filing_stage.needs: a dedicated AppFolio eviction-filing/stage source (see master plan §5.7 deep pipeline)

court_dates.needs: a dedicated AppFolio court-date source (see master plan §5.7 deep pipeline)
